courses.description: A longtime fixture near the top of our Best in Alaska list, Anchorage Golf Course offers stunning views of Denali—the tallest mountain in North America—and the downtown skyline. At just 6,600 yards from the tips, it’s not a long course, but the fairways are closely guarded by thick pines. The course plays along hilly terrain and features subtle doglegs, requiring proper shot placement to avoid being blocked by the lurking pines. It’s not unusual to have moose strolling the fairways. After hosting the 2022 U.S. Senior Women's Amateur, the USGA has now hosted one of its championships in all 50 U.S. states.

The Creek course at Moose Run—America’s northernmost 36-hole facility—is currently ranked No. 2 on our Best in Alaska list. Narrow fairways and strategic bunkering demand quality ball-striking, especially on the split fairways at the fourth and fifth holes. This military course is adjacent to Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson and is set at the base of the Chugach Mountains, offering impressive views.

courses.description: Driving up, this public course in the center of Golden, Colo., seems to hold little promise. The locale is a hodgepodge of urban blight, with remains of a 19th-century clay pit mine edged by a high school, juvenile detention center, water park and fast-food joints. Which is why Jim Engh was the perfect architect to design Fossil Trace. His style, with recessed fairways and greens that twist, turn and funnel shots toward the center, dips beneath the urban landscape and places it out of view. What’s more, Engh’s emotional architecture aims to surprise and delight golfers. The back nine is one-of-a-kind, with the 11th through 15th playing beneath sandstone bluffs and rock monoliths, particularly on the 12th, where walls of fossils split the fairway and hide the green. Even the fishhook par-5 18th, all grass and water and no rock, is intriguing. Any time a course exceeds expectations, it’s a fun day of golf.

The Robert Trent Jones Sr.-designed South Course is routed among the rolling terrain of northern Delaware and provides some really good vistas of large sections of the golf course. The routing takes advantage of the terrain to produce a course with strong layout design and a variety of holes that include thoughtful short par-4s, such as the 11th, challenging longer par-4s such as No. 5 and a beautiful mix of par-3s. Tree removal and some tornado damage about 15 years ago opened up the course even more. Additionally, renovation work by Andrew Green has brought the look and infrastructure up to 21st-century ideals. The South Course hosted the 2022 BMW Championship, won by Patrick Cantlay, the first time the PGA Tour hosted an event in Delaware.

courses.description: A majestic Donald Ross design with a clever routing on a rectangular site, each hole at Seminole encounters a different wind direction. Ben Hogan used to spend the weeks leading up to the Masters at Seminole, for the Ross greens were the perfect prep for Augusta. He once said, "If you can play Seminole, you can play any course in the world."

TPC’s stadium concept was the idea of then-PGA Tour commissioner Deane Beman. The 1980 design was pioneering for its spectator mounds, and for its diverse variety of holes that don’t favor any one type of player. It’s most famous for the island-green 17th hole, but the finishing stretch of 16-17-18 is one of the most exciting in golf.

Calusa’s developer, Gary Chensoff, a Chicago venture capitalist, survived a rare form of cancer despite long odds, and the name of the club and its logo (a stylized logo of the rare Pinus glabra, or Spruce Pine) are a tribute to his recovery. The course, designed by Dana Fry and Michael Hurdzan, is famous for its massive land-moving project that created dramatic elevation changes in a flat landscape.

As an old pro from Pine Valley who lost an Open at Merion, George Fazio blended features of both of those courses into Jupiter Hills. The course is set on a massive ridge of sand that provides uncharacteristic elevation changes for Florida.

The Concession was originally established by Sarasota resident Tony Jacklin, who convinced Jack Nicklaus to collaborate on the design. The name commemorates the famous 1969 Ryder Cup "concession" where Nicklaus conceded a putt to Jacklin, resulting in the first tie in Ryder Cup history.

Coore and Crenshaw’s Red Course is part of a resort triple-header that gives golfers a rare opportunity to play different styles of golf in one location. The Red course is routed through the most dramatic dunes and water-filled pits of the property.

The Dye Course at White Oak, our 2022 Best New Private Course winner, is one of the most exclusive and unique golf experiences in the country. Designed by Pete Dye and Bobby Weed, it's located on a massive conservation property.

Featuring picturesque views of the White Tank Mountains, the Founders course is a very playable John Fought and Tom Lehman design. Five sets of tee boxes and generous landing areas, coupled with hazardous rock formations and elevation changes, make this course enjoyable for a variety of skill levels.

The Robert Trent Jones Sr. designed Gold Course is the standout among the three courses at the Wigwam—and it's ranked in the top 25 of public courses in the state. The Gold Course was renovated in 2015 and plays plenty long at 7,345 yards from the back tees, with narrow fairways and strategically positioned bunkers along with small greens.

One of two 18-hole layouts designed by Tom Fazio on property, the Canyon course incorporates the natural desert landscape into its design, with plenty of desert vegetation and wildlife. The course is known for its challenging greens and scenic mountain backdrops.

Tucked in a canyon 30 minutes northeast of Phoenix Sky Harbor, Sunridge Canyon features an intriguing layout that descends through the canyon on the front nine and climbs back up on the back nine. The final six holes, known as the \"Wicked Six,\" provide a challenging finish.

Designed by Jay Morrish, this par-71 championship course is known for its natural beauty and intricate design that weaves through ancient rock formations. The course is part of The Boulders Resort & Spa and offers a unique desert golf experience.

courses.course_name: Paradise Valley Country Club: Paradise Valley

courses.description: Host of the PGA Tour’s WGC match-play event in 2007 and 2008, the South course at Gallery Golf Club features wide fairways and large greens. The layout is more traditional than its sister North course but still offers plenty of desert challenges.

Designed by Tom Fazio, the Raptor Course is one of two 18-hole layouts at Grayhawk. The course, which hosted the Frys.com Open between 2007-'09, features generous fairways and greens, and has been lauded for excellent conditioning and service.

An annual U.S. Open qualifying site and host to several top collegiate tournaments, Ak-Chin Southern Dunes features a links-style layout with deep bunkers and wide-open fairways. It's known for its challenging but fair test of golf.

The famed home of the WM Phoenix Open boasts probably the most well-known stadium hole in golf: the par-3 16th. Designed by Jay Morrish and Tom Weiskopf, the Stadium course is a desert layout that becomes a massive amphitheater during tournament week.

The Lost Gold course is one of two Jack Nicklaus designs at Superstition Mountain, a private club located at the base of the Superstition Mountains. The layout is a desert-style course with generous fairways and large greens.

Desert courses are rarely walkable, mainly because of great distances between greens and tee boxes, but Talking Rock is an exception. Designed by Jay Morrish, the course plays through rolling hills and high-desert vegetation.

Though our panelists rank the Cholla course as the second best at We-Ko-Pa, it's in the top-10 among public courses in the state. Designed by Scott Miller, Cholla is a pure desert layout with no homes or development surrounding the course.

The short par-3 third hole, just a wedge even from the back tees, features a tiny target between rock outcroppings. Designed by Tom Fazio, the Mountain course is a rugged desert layout with significant elevation changes.

Currently ranked among our Best Courses in Arizona, Blackstone is a desert-style course with many elevation changes and scenic views. The course was designed by Jim Engh and features his signature deep bunkers and undulating greens.

Like its sister course, the Pinnacle at Troon North is carved into the natural desert landscape with many forced carries and dramatic rock outcroppings. Designed by Tom Weiskopf, it's a quintessential Scottsdale desert golf experience.

The North course at Gallery Golf Club features plenty of elevation changes and offers beautiful panoramic views of the Tortolita Mountains. Designed by John Fought and Tom Lehman, it's a rugged desert layout.

The Nicklaus-designed Outlaw course is a links-style layout with generous fairways, deep-faced pot bunkers, and undulating greens. It's the highest-elevation course at Desert Mountain, offering cooler temperatures and great views.

Playing among the stately ponderosa pines in Flagstaff, this Jerry Pate design has plenty of elevation changes and is a true mountain golf course. It offers a cool escape from the desert heat in the summer.

This Tom Fazio design is an aesthetically pleasing desert layout with generous fairways that play gently through the natural terrain. It's known for its excellent conditioning and scenic views of Pinnacle Peak.

Formerly home to the PGA Tour’s WGC match-play event, the Saguaro and Tortolita nines feature guarded greens and wide desert fairways. Designed by Jack Nicklaus, the course is a rugged and challenging test.
courses.course_name: Golf Club At Dove Mountain: Saguaro/Tortolita

courses.description: Jack Nicklaus not only designed the Cochise course at Desert Mountain Club but also won the major on the Senior Tour here several times. The layout features signature rock outcroppings and challenging approach shots.

About an hour and a half northeast of Phoenix, Chaparral Pines is a private mountain course designed by David Graham and Gary Panks. It plays through thick stands of pine trees and features significant elevation changes.

The manageable summer temperatures at high elevation on this par-71 championship golf course allow for year-round play. Designed by Bill Brownlee and Wendell Pickett, it's a desert-style layout with dramatic elevation changes.

This esteemed Tom Weiskopf design pays homage to Open Championship-style golf in the middle of the Arizona desert. The course features a massive boulder in the middle of the third fairway, giving the course its name.

A previous honoree on Golf Digest's 100 Greatest Public, the Saguaro course is ranked among the best public options in the state. Designed by Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw, it's a more traditional, walkable layout in a desert setting.

Situated in the Coconino National Forest in Sedona, Seven Canyons is a picturesque desert layout with dramatic red-rock backdrops. Designed by Tom Weiskopf, it's one of the most scenic courses in the country.

Designed by Jay Morrish and Tom Weiskopf in the mid-1980s, Troon Country Club was one of the first high-end private clubs in the Scottsdale area. The course is a classic desert layout with challenging greens.

The Rim Golf Club sits about an hour-and-a-half northeast of Scottsdale, and unlike many traditional desert courses, it plays through pine trees at a higher elevation. Designed by Jay Morrish and Tom Weiskopf, it's a rugged mountain layout.

Perhaps no course in the greater Phoenix area provides a better experience of the area's diverse topography. Designed by Rees Jones, Quintero features massive elevation changes and dramatic desert views.

We named Renegade one of the most important designs of its decade in a magazine feature in 2010 for its unique \"two-pin\" system on every green, allowing for multiple configurations and levels of difficulty. Designed by Jack Nicklaus.
The Mineshaft course is the original of the three courses at Scottsdale National, previously known as Golf Club of Scottsdale. Designed by Jay Morrish and Dick Huish, it's a rugged desert layout.

Arguably the most visually intimidating of the seven courses at Desert Mountain Club, Geronimo is a target-style desert course designed by Jack Nicklaus. It features many forced carries over desert washes.

One of six Jack Nicklaus-designed courses at Desert Mountain, Chiricahua ranges in elevation from 3,000 to 3,300 feet. The course features many elevation changes and is known for its dramatic mountain views.
The Tom Weiskopf-designed Meadow course at Forest Highlands is a parkland layout that plays among the pine trees. It's more open and forgiving than its sister Canyon course but still a top-tier test.

A couple of miles from the Tom Weiskopf-designed Stadium course at TPC Scottsdale, the private Silverleaf Club is a Tom Weiskopf design that plays into the McDowell Mountains. It's known for its luxury and scenic beauty.

Host of the first two televised Skins Games in 1983 and 1984, featuring Nicklaus, Palmer, Player and Watson, Desert Highlands is a landmark design by Jack Nicklaus that helped define the desert golf style.
Widely considered to be the first desert course ever built, Desert Forest was designed by Robert “Red” Lawrence in 1962. The course is a pure desert layout that rewards accurate shot-making.

The Stone Canyon Club, which climbs the slopes of the Tortolita Mountains north of Tucson, is considered Jay Morrish's masterpiece. The course features massive granite boulders and dramatic elevation changes.

This luxury mountain community in Flagstaff features a championship golf course consistently ranked among the best in the state. Designed by Jay Morrish, it plays through pine forests with scenic mountain views.

Phil Mickelson wanted his course design debut to be something different than the typical Scottsdale target-golf style. Designed by Mickelson and Gary Panks, the Lower course is a more traditional desert layout.

courses.description: Whisper Rock’s Upper Course was intended, as the club’s second 18, to specifically test its low-handicap membership. Designed by Tom Fazio, it's a rugged and challenging desert layout.

courses.description: The Other Course, built by David Kahn and Tim Jackson, is the second 18-hole course at Scottsdale National. It's a rugged, links-inspired layout that contrasts with the club's Mineshaft course.

It has three par 5s over 600 yards and a par 4 measuring 478 yards, but the Canyon Course at Forest Highlands is more than just length. Designed by Tom Weiskopf and Jay Morrish, it's a top-tier mountain course.

Estancia, our Best New Private Course of 1996, was Tom Fazio’s initial entry into the Scottsdale scene. The course winds through the boulders at the base of Pinnacle Peak and is known for its impeccable conditioning.

courses.description: Built in 2002 by Tom Fazio, Squire Creek is a par 72 that stretches to over 7,000 yards with multiple par-3s over 190 yards. In his 2017 Christmas update to The Confidential Guide to Golf Courses, Tom Doak said: “The I-20 corridor across northern Louisiana is one of America’s major truck routes, so it only made sense for James Davidson to run his trucking firm out of there, and eventually, a Tom Fazio golf course for his employees and for a regional membership. Cut through an undulating oak and pine forest, Squire Creek’s front nine is some of Tom Fazio’s best work, with two arms of the creek coming into play on nearly every hole from the second to the eighth.”
The Country Club of Louisiana in Baton Rouge is one of the best courses in Louisiana. Discover our experts’ reviews and where The Country Club of Louisiana sits in our rankings.

courses.description: Metairie Country Club, opened in 1925, was the jewel of New Orleans golf through the first decade of its existence. It was designed by Seth Raynor and possessed variations of his much-studied template holes originally introduced by his mentor, C.B. Macdonald, at National Golf Links of America and other courses. A land swap in 1928 fundamentally altered the second nine routing of holes 14-18, and hard economic conditions and infrastructure challenges in the late 1930s prompted further renovations that compromised Raynor's design, a trend that continued for decades. The club never retained Raynor's blueprints for the course and wasn't sure precisely what the architect had left behind. So, in 2021, they hired Brian Silva to reimagine a pure Raynor course on the property. Drawing on as much experience studying and reviving Raynor's designs throughout the country as anyone over the last 30 years, Silva reproduced vivid versions of the Bottle, Biarritz, Redan, Leven, Road, Eden and others. Though not a historical restoration, Metairie has been completely and imaginatively transformed.

courses.description: Home of the Zurich Classic of New Orleans since 2007, this Pete Dye design exudes a flair of TPC Sawgrass with some more low-profile design features like hidden bunkers and green complexes that offer options on approach. Dye had help from fellow tour pros Steve Elkington and New Orleans native Kelly Gibson on this 7,400-yard par-72 layout. Consistently ranked in the top 10 of Golf Digest’s Best in State, TPC Louisiana offers a nice mix of challenging short and long holes.

courses.description: This 7,000-yard, par-72 features beautifully manicured Bermuda greens, and minimal trees that make room for open and forgiving fairways. The course can become quite challenging as the winds pick up from the Gulf of Mexico just a few miles away. With multiple tee options and an eclectic mix of long and short par-3s, par-4s and par-5s, the Golden Nugget course is an intriguing feat for golfers of all handicap levels.
courses.course_name: The Country Club At Golden Nugget

courses.description: Prairie Dunes was the top nine-hole course in America for 20 years. By the time the club found funds to expand it to 18, original architect Perry Maxwell had passed away, but his son Press was able to add nine more holes seamlessly in the 1950s, putting three on the front nine and six on the back. He also replicated his father’s great greens, which seem to break in three different directions. Prairie Dunes reflects all that is common in rural Kansas: sand dunes, prairie grasses, yucca plants, cottonwoods and constant wind.

Located 15 miles east of Wichita on the western perimeter of its namesake Flint Hills prairie region, Flint Hills National looks and plays like a course that’s been there for decades. Tom Fazio’s layout is a masterful blend of the rolling prairie terrain and the dense stands of hardwoods that line many of the holes. The course features several water hazards, including a large lake that comes into play on the par-4 18th hole.

Debuting as Golf Digest’s Best New Private Course in 1989, Shadow Glen plays along rocky ridges and through tree-lined valleys. The course sits less than a half-hour from Kansas City and presents an array of challenges, including stone-lined water hazards, rugged ravines and undulating greens. The Tom Weiskopf design is a past member of our 100 Greatest list, and has been ranked inside the top five on our Best in Kansas list since 1991.
Kansas City Country Club is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Kansas. Discover our experts' reviews and photos, and see where it ranks on our current Best in State list.
Wolf Creek in Olathe is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Kansas. Discover our experts' reviews and photos, and see where it ranks on our current Best in State list.
Hallbrook Country Club in Leawood is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Kansas. Discover our experts' reviews and photos, and see where it ranks on our current Best in State list.
Milburn Country Club in Overland Park is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Kansas. Discover our experts' reviews and photos, and see where it ranks on our current Best in State list.

Designed by eight-time PGA Tour winner Jim Colbert along with architect Jeff Brauer, Colbert Hills is the highest-ranked public course in the state and ranked in the top 10 in our Best in Kansas rankings. Colbert, a decorated collegiate golfer who finished runner-up at NCAAs during his time at Kansas State, included a fun element as an homage to his alma mater on the fifth hole, with a bunker complex in the shape of a wildcat paw print (in honor of the school's mascot). On big football weekends, the facility will make the sand purple. Colbert Hills is a challenging yet playable layout with strategic hazards but generous fairways and large putting surfaces, which average over 7,000 square feet. The rolling terrain offers terrific views of the beautiful Flint Hills region framed by native prairie tallgrass. In addition to being home of the Kansas State men’s and women’s golf teams, it also houses the First Tee of Manhattan, including the Earl Woods National Youth Golf Academy, named for Tiger's father who was born in Manhattan, played baseball at K-State and was the first Black baseball player in the Big Seven conference (now the Big 12).
Wichita Country Club is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Kansas. Discover our experts' reviews and photos, and see where it ranks on our current Best in State list.

In 2014, Sand Creek Station hosted the final U.S. Amateur Public Links, a championship first played in 1922. Byron Meth captured the title in 2014, defeating Doug Ghim in extra holes. The course, located about 30 minutes north of Wichita, is bisected by a railway and features several template holes, including the Road Hole and Redan.

TPC Potomac At Avenel Farm is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Maryland. Originally designed by Ed Ault, Tom Clark and Ed Sneed in 1986, it was completely rebuilt by PGA Tour Design Services in 2008.
Four Streams in Beallsville is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Maryland. A stout player's course from Steve Smyers and Nick Price with sweeping cape and bay bunkers.

courses.description: The Links at Perry Cabin is a late-career Pete Dye design that roams through a community near the banks of the state’s eastern bay. Assisted by his younger son, P.B., Pete transformed a low-profile 1971 collaboration with brother Roy.
Whiskey Creek Golf Club in Ijamsville is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Maryland. Designed by Ernie Els and J. Michael Poellot.

Though barely two decades old, The Harvester Club has led an adventurous life. It came into the world at the end of the 1990s as a course of its time: that is, an upscale daily-fee design 30 minutes northeast of Des Moines with snaking fairways and curvaceous, modern-looking bunkering. A renovation in 2010 began to alter their character, roughing up the edges and giving the course a more rustic look. In 2017, the owners reversed course and took the club private, hiring original architect Keith Foster to remove trees to better highlight the site's hills and prairie terrain and to revamp the holes with new tees and wider, less snaking fairways. Foster also reimagined the course shifting new flat-bottomed grass-faced bunkers to more interesting and impactful locations, and adding thematic riffs on a Road Hole green, a Tillinghast-inspired Hell's Half Acre, a Short Hole and an Oakmont-like Church Pews bunker.

Davenport Country Club architect Charles Alison might have been the most traveled architect of his time. Based in London, he designed courses in the U.K., Japan, Africa, Europe and the U.S., including Milwaukee Country Club (ranked 97th on America's 100 Greatest Courses), Kirtland near Cleveland and Burning Tree outside Washington D.C. The course sits on a beautiful, ruptured piece of land near the Mississippi River.

courses.other_details: Originally 9 holes by Bendelow; Ross expanded to 18 in 1914-1915.
courses.architect: Donald Ross (18 holes expansion), Tom Bendelow (initial 9 holes); Restored by Ron Prichard and Tyler Rae
courses.description: For decades, Cedar Rapids Country Club had the feel of a classic parkland design, with sturdy rough and small, tree-pinched greens. But a multi-year restoration by Ron Prichard and Tyler Rae has revealed the brilliance of its Donald Ross bones. The course now features wide corridors, expansive bunkers, and the intricate, large-scale green complexes that Ross was known for. It is the only Donald Ross design in Iowa.

courses.course_name: Des Moines Golf and Country Club: North
courses.other_details: Part of a 36-hole Pete Dye complex.
courses.architect: Pete Dye, ASGCA; Renovated by Pete Dye and Tim Liddy in 2014
courses.description: Des Moines Golf and Country Club North Course is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Iowa. It moved to its current site in 1969 when Pete Dye designed both courses. The North is the longer and more difficult layout. It hosted the 1999 U.S. Senior Open and the 2017 Solheim Cup (as part of a composite course).

Located on a beautiful property cut with ravines and depressions in south Des Moines, Wakonda is a classic design from the 1920s. It features the bold, geometric bunkering and massive, multi-tiered greens that were a hallmark of the Langford and Moreau partnership. A restoration by Tyler Rae has helped to recapture the scale and intent of the original design.

Glen Oaks Country Club in West Des Moines is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Iowa. Developed on over 500 acres of farmland beginning in 1991, the course was designed by Tom Fazio. It was the first gated residential community in central Iowa.
courses.architect: Tom Fazio; Renovated by Dennis Wise in 2016-2017

courses.description: Spirit Hollow in Burlington is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Iowa. Designed by Rick Jacobson, it features rolling hills and natural streams. The course was a vision of local golfer Randy Winegard who acquired the land in 1991.
courses.other_details: True hidden gem with on-site lodging.

courses.description: Des Moines Golf and Country Club South Course is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Iowa. One of the two courses on the property designed by Pete Dye early in his career. It features pot bunkers and characteristic Dye design elements.
courses.course_name: Des Moines Golf and Country Club: South
courses.architect: Pete Dye, ASGCA; (R) Richard Jacobson, ASGCA

courses.description: Built atop Peabody Coal Company’s long-abandoned Victoria strip mine in southern Indiana, Victoria National is one of Tom Fazio’s most challenging and scenic designs, with deep ravines and water hazards created by the mining excavations.

Crooked Stick is the course where Pete Dye became Pete Dye. Conceived following an extended tour of Scotland, it features the pot bunkers, railroad ties and small, contoured greens that would become his signatures.

courses.course_name: French Lick Resort: Pete Dye Course
courses.description: Pete Dye’s mountaintop design, Golf Digest’s 2009 Best New Public winner, established that at age 83 he was still as creative and unconventional as ever. The course offers spectacular views from its high ridges.

Jack Nicklaus has redesigned some aspects of every hole at Muirfield Village over the decades, and Sycamore Hills reflects his evolving design philosophy with its emphasis on strategy and use of the natural river features.

College golf courses can be the most challenging of assignments for architects because of the need to balance the requirements of a championship-caliber team with those of the general student body. Steve Smyers succeeded brilliantly here.
courses.course_name: The Pfau Course At Indiana University

courses.description: It's fitting that one of the country's best college courses should be built by college students. Purdue students helped with the design and construction of this Pete Dye masterpiece.
courses.course_name: Birck Boilermaker Golf Complex: Kampen Course

courses.description: Given there is very little elevation change on Notre Dame’s Warren Golf Course, it was an impressive feat for Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw to create such a subtle and strategic masterpiece that looks like it has been there for decades.
courses.course_name: Warren Golf Course at Notre Dame

courses.description: If the 2009 Pete Dye course at this historic resort is the flashy new star, the Donald Ross Course is the steady, classic foundation. Renovated in 2005, it remains one of the purest examples of Ross's work.
courses.course_name: French Lick Resort: Donald Ross Course

courses.description: As an unapologetic Pete Dye fan, I know the most economical way to understand and appreciate Dye's genius is to play this course, which features four holes inside the iconic Indianapolis Motor Speedway oval.

courses.course_name: Holliday Farms Golf Club: Championship Course
courses.description: This private golf and residential development outside of Indianapolis is one of the last courses Pete Dye personally worked on, featuring his signature strategic challenges and visual drama.
Rock Hollow Golf Club is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Indiana. Discover our experts' reviews and tee time information.
The Ackerman-Allen course at Purdue’s 36-hole Birck Boilermaker Golf Complex was originally designed by Bill Diddel and later redesigned by Pete Dye to complement the neighboring Kampen course.
Woodland Country Club of Carmel is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Indiana. Discover our experts' reviews and tee time information.
The Club At Chatham Hills is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Indiana. Discover our experts' reviews and tee time information.
The Fort Golf Resort is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Indiana. Discover our experts' reviews and tee time information.
South Bend Country Club is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Indiana. Discover our experts' reviews and tee time information.
The Sagamore Club is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Indiana. Discover our experts' reviews and tee time information.
Prairie View Golf Club is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Indiana. Discover our experts' reviews and tee time information.
Purgatory Golf Club is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Indiana. Discover our experts' reviews and tee time information.
The Trophy Club is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Indiana. Discover our experts' reviews and tee time information.

courses.architect: Bruce Hepner (Redesign); Stiles and Van Kleek (Original)
courses.description: Boothbay Harbor Country Club is a semi-private club on the coast of Maine offering stay-and-play packages at its cottages, villas, and oceanside resort. It underwent a $50 million makeover in 2015 and 2016, funded by philanthropist owner Paul Coulombe.
courses.other_details: Redesigned in 2015-16; known for being one of the best-conditioned courses in the state; features significant elevation changes and water features; best enjoyed in a cart. Previous rank: 2.
Length: 5,859 yards. Strategy-focused with small green complexes. Previous rank: 1.
courses.description: Despite its relatively short length, Cape Arundel offers a significant challenge, particularly around its small, undulating greens. It has remained true to its Golden Age design roots.
A picturesque design in central Maine, Belgrade Lakes is noted for its scenic practice green and a layout featuring elevation changes, strategic doglegs, and omnipresent boulders.
courses.other_details: Includes a massive, undulating double green shared by the 9th and 18th holes. Previous rank: 3.

courses.description: An exclusive private course just north of downtown Portland with views of Casco Bay. The club restored many original Donald Ross features in 2017.
courses.other_details: Restored in 2017; features sloped greens and a mix of exposed and tree-lined holes. Previous rank: 5.
Signature holes 10 and 11 offer dramatic dropoffs; 11th hole is nicknamed 'Precipice' with a 120-foot drop. Previous rank: 4.
courses.description: A demanding mountain course set in the Carrabassett Valley, Sugarloaf features narrow fairways guarded by forest and rock, and undulating greens with numerous shelves.

courses.other_details: Yardage: 6,131. Slope: 123. Features the 'Taft' hole (17th), named after President William Taft who once carded a 27 there. Previous rank: 6.
courses.description: Established in 1888, Kebo Valley is one of the oldest clubs in the U.S. and borders Acadia National Park. It is known for its small, undulating greens and historical significance.

Set amongst the Appalachians, Sunday River offers scenic mountain vistas and more generous landing areas than its 'sibling' Sugarloaf. It is especially noted for its late September fall foliage experience.
courses.other_details: Yardage: 7,130. Slope: 150. Price: $207. Located near a top Maine ski resort. Previous rank: 1.

courses.architect: Bob Aldred; (R) Geoffrey S. Cornish; Brad Booth
courses.description: Known as the 'Pebble Beach of the East,' Samoset is located on the Maine coast with views of the Atlantic Ocean on 14 of its 18 holes.
courses.other_details: Yardage: 6,548. Slope: 133. Price: $200. Features several holes along Penobscot Bay, including a coastal par-5 4th hole. Previous rank: NR.

courses.description: Perry Maxwell, the Midwest associate of architect Alister MacKenzie, lived on site while constructing the course. It is ranked 1st in the state and among the top in the country.

courses.other_details: Ranked 2nd in the state; previously ranked 2nd.
courses.description: Donald Ross felt his 1918 design was out of date for the 1951 U.S. Open and was prepared to remodel it. The course has a long history of hosting major championships.
The Bluffs Course at Arcadia Bluffs has been a high-ranking course, sometimes considered a sleeper despite its high position.
Expertly routed across glacial domes and over kettle holes, Kingsley Club opens with a split fairway.
Mentioned in connection with Mike Keiser’s terrific nine-hole Dunes Club in New Buffalo, Mich.
A Tom Weiskopf-designed layout described as terrific on a terrific piece of property, with portions resembling Augusta National.
Designed to create a unique challenge at the Arcadia Bluffs resort.
Created by architect Mike DeVries, showing dramatic design on the Upper Peninsula.

courses.course_name: Point O' Woods Golf & Country Club
courses.description: Ranked as one of the best golf courses in Michigan.
Captures the best of Northern Michigan golf.
A reversible golf course, the clockwise routing of The Loop.
courses.course_name: The Loop Black Course at Forest Dunes

courses.description: Ranked as one of the best golf courses in the country.
First six holes designed by Willie Park, Jr. in 1916. Harry Collis and Jack Daray were also involved.
courses.architect: Willie Park, Jr., Harry Collis, Jack Daray

courses.description: Steve Smyers routed the course on a hilly site in an old cherry orchard with views of Lake Michigan.
The counterclockwise routing of the reversible The Loop course.
Features exciting layout variety with five par-3s (implied).

courses.architect: Lynn Lavis, Bill Diddel, Arthur Hills
courses.description: Originally designed with the help of Lynn Lavis, Bill Diddel, and later Arthur Hills to improve drainage and routing.
The Donald Ross design often overshadowed by its South course counterpart.
courses.course_name: Oakland Hills Country Club North Course

courses.description: One of three grand layouts at the club.
A scenic Jack Nicklaus layout located 90 minutes from Chicago.
courses.course_name: The Golf Club at Harbor Shores

courses.description: Designed by the duo responsible for the original Arcadia Bluffs.
Designed by William Watson, whose career began in Minnesota.
Located near prominent clubs like Oakland Hills and Bloomfield Hills.
Ranked as one of the best golf courses in Michigan.
One of four 18-hole layouts at The Highlands.
A total reinvention of an existing course, opened in 2021.
Designed by Alister MacKenzie, one of a few college courses he designed.
Offers 27 holes of championship golf in central Michigan.
courses.course_name: Eagle Eye Golf & Banquet Center: Hawk Hollow

courses.description: Donald Ross praised the property's potential to the founding members.
Ranked as one of the best golf courses in Michigan.
Crafted by English golf professional Wilfred Reid and his partner.
Sits at the center of one of the country's historic golf regions.

courses.course_name: Grand Traverse Resort and Spa: The Bear
courses.description: One of three championship layouts at the resort, ranked for 10 years on the 100 Greatest Public list.
Described as the most player-friendly of the four layouts at The Highlands.
Ranked as one of the best golf courses in Michigan.
courses.course_name: Battle Creek Country Club: Battle Creek

courses.description: One of two 18-hole courses designed by Donald Ross at the club.
A 27-hole public facility with a northern Michigan feel.
Blends playability with character, featuring wide fairways and large greens.
One of six 18-hole courses at the Gull Lake View Golf Resort.
courses.course_name: Gull Lake View Golf Resort: Stoatin Brae

courses.description: A Pete and Alice Dye design described as Dye in his gentlest form, located at the University of Michigan.

Set in the Rocky Mountain foothills, this inland links course was fashioned from a working cattle ranch. It features a broad routing that climbs from pasture to pines, crossing the ravines of Rock Creek before returning to rolling hills with unique fairway features like hogbacks and punchbowls.

Located at 7,500 feet, this is the highest altitude course in the rankings. Built on an old ski mountain, it offers 360-degree Rocky Mountain views. The design features long, dramatic holes, including a 700+ yard par 5, and requires precise distance control in the thin mountain air.
courses.other_details: Highest altitude in the rankings (7,500 feet)

courses.description: Located in the Bitterroot Valley, this course offers stunning views of the Sapphire Mountains. The layout makes use of the dramatic landscape with numerous elevated tees. The club also offers outdoor activities like fly-fishing and horseback riding.

Situated between Big Mountain and Whitefish Lake, this picturesque course winds through dense forests with significant elevation changes. The routing follows the natural terrain, often featuring local wildlife on its sloping fairways and bentgrass greens.

Located near the Canadian border, this course features quick-draining sandy soil and free-form waste bunkers. It is surrounded by three mountain ranges and features several lakes, including Grob Lake which impacts the finishing holes.

A scenic mountain course set at 7,000 feet above sea level, providing panoramic views of the forest and mountains. The layout is demanding off the tee, with fairways guarded by native grasses and dense woods.

Built on the site of a former copper mine, this course features unique black slag in its bunkers as a nod to its industrial history. It offers high-end conditioning at an affordable price, with an iconic 585-foot smelter stack visible from the course.

Features two distinct nines: a front nine designed in the Scottish links style and a back nine that navigates more intense elevation and follows the Stillwater River. Standout features include wide driving areas and fast, challenging greens.
The golf course wasn’t so much designed as discovered. Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw trudged back and forth across the land for years before they finally settled on the routing.

Located in north-central Nebraska, the course makes its debut on Golf Digest’s list of America’s 100 Greatest Courses at No. 72. The layout features dramatic elevation changes and views of the Snake River canyon.

courses.architect: Rob Collins and Tad King (King-Collins Golf Course Design)
courses.description: A new design in northeastern Nebraska that features massive scale and bold features, located about 10 miles from Sioux City. It debuted in the top 200 of Golf Digest's rankings.

Located just southwest of Omaha, this course was a homecoming project for Scottsdale-based architect Scott Hoffman. It features a layout built through a variety of terrains including woodlands and open areas.
Architect David McLay Kidd designed this course on a site that offers a pure sandhills experience. It is part of the Dormie Network and focuses on a high-end, private golf experience.
Tom Doak created this companion 18 to the Jack Nicklaus-designed White Course, offering a different but equally compelling sandhills experience.
The Dunes Course flows through a rumpled blanket landscape of the rugged, treeless sandhills, providing a pure links-style experience.
Opened in 1899, it is one of the oldest clubs in the Midwest. The course has some intense elevation changes and has hosted major championships like the U.S. Senior Open.
Named for the eponymous body of water that flows through the rural Nebraska site, it was the first course at the club and features Nicklaus's signature challenging design.
The only course in Nebraska designed by the late Pete Dye. It features his characteristic bunkering and challenging green complexes.
A public “little brother” to Sand Hills, it is frequently cited as one of the best low-cost golf courses in the nation, built on ideal sandy soil.
The Pines Course has a mix of sandhills holes and holes that play through and around a canyon of ponderosa pines, offering a distinct contrast to the Dunes Course.
Lauded for its environmental sensitivity, ArborLinks was built in open plains and is a key property in the Dormie Network.
Built in and around an old rock quarry, the course features dramatic elevation changes and a variety of terrain including deep woods and rocky outcrops.
Located on Santee Sioux Nation land, the course was designed to reflect an intense bond with the natural environment and Native American culture. It opened in 2015.
courses.course_name: Tatanka Golf Club at Feather Hill
The Country Club: The Main Course
courses.description: The Country Club’s 18-hole course, which was the scene of the 1963 and 1988 U.S. Opens, is not the same as the one on which it hosted the 2022 U.S. Open (won by Matt Fitzpatrick). For major championships, the club uses a composite course consisting of holes from its primary 18 (the Clyde and Squirrel) and its third nine, the Primrose. For day-to-day play, the Clyde and Squirrel are the main courses. The Country Club is a classic, old-school layout with small greens, narrow fairways, and a premium on shot-making and accuracy.

Few realize Myopia Hunt Club, a funky, quirky lark where greens look like bathmats and bunkers look like they were dug by hand, was actually the scene of four U.S. Opens in the early 20th century. Designed by Herbert Leeds, it remains one of the most unique and challenging tests of golf in the country, despite its relatively modest length. The course is known for its penal bunkers, blind shots, and intricate green complexes.

Old Sandwich Golf Club may be the craftiest Coore-Crenshaw design yet built. Amidst its pines, scrub, and sand, the architects created a layout that feels like it has been there for a century. The course features wide fairways that require strategic positioning to approach the large, contoured greens. It is a masterful example of modern-day classic architecture.

Essex County Club is considered the first great Donald Ross design and perhaps his most intriguing. Ross served as the professional here and refined the course over many years. It features dramatic elevation changes, bold bunkering, and some of the most complex and interesting greens in Ross's portfolio. The back nine is particularly noted for its rugged, rocky terrain.

Only recently, with the discovery of some original blueprints, has it been conclusively established that William Flynn was the primary architect of Kittansett. Located on a point jutting into Buzzards Bay, the course is defined by its seaside setting, wind, and sandy soil. The par-3 third hole, with its green surrounded by sand and water, is one of the most famous in New England.

Boston Golf Club, in the southern suburb of Hingham, is a modern-day Pine Valley, massaged by architect Gil Hanse into a rugged, sandy site. The layout is wild and dramatic, with significant elevation changes and natural-looking bunkering. It is a walking-only club that emphasizes the traditional values of the game.

This is Herbert Fowler's most engaging 18-hole design. Routed on an isthmus in the Atlantic on Cape Cod, the course features some of the most dramatic and undulating fairways in golf. The wind is a constant factor, and the views of Pleasant Bay are spectacular. Recent restoration work has returned the course to its original, rugged character.

Some of America’s greatest golf courses were designed by first-time novices and non-architects: Merion by Hugh Wilson, Pine Valley by George Crump, and Sankaty Head on Nantucket by Emerson Armstrong. Located near the island's famous lighthouse, the course features a links-like feel with wide-open spaces and firm, fast conditions.

Massachusetts is ground zero for Donald Ross-designed courses. Worcester is one of his finest, having hosted the first Ryder Cup in 1927. The course is built on rolling, hilly terrain and features Ross's signature green complexes and strategic bunkering. It has long been a standard for classic New England golf.

Salem was a mainstay on the Golf Digest ranking of America’s 100 Greatest Courses since the list’s inception. Another Donald Ross masterpiece, it has hosted several national championships, including the U.S. Senior Open. The course is known for its challenging greens and beautiful, forested setting.

Winchester Country Club is another highly regarded Donald Ross design in the Boston area. The course is situated on a hilly site with significant elevation changes, requiring precise shot-making and careful club selection. The greens are notoriously fast and difficult to read.

Concord Country Club is ranked as one of the best golf courses in Massachusetts. This Donald Ross design features a classic New England feel with rolling fairways and intricate green complexes. The course has a long history and is known for its well-maintained conditions and strategic challenge.

Taconic dates back to 1896 and is the home course of the Williams College men’s and women’s golf teams. It is a classic parkland layout with beautiful views of the surrounding Berkshire Mountains. The course was renovated by Gil Hanse and is consistently ranked among the top public courses in the country.

Gil Hanse's team completed renovation on the 12th and 13th holes at TPC Boston in 2017, completing a multi-year effort to return the course to a more natural, rugged look. Formerly a regular stop on the PGA Tour, the course features a mix of challenging holes and beautiful landscaping.

Charles River Country Club in Newton Centre is another exceptional Donald Ross design. The course is characterized by its narrow fairways, small greens, and strategic bunkering. It has a rich history of hosting significant amateur and professional tournaments.

Oyster Harbors Club in Osterville is a classic Donald Ross layout located on an island on Cape Cod. The course features beautiful water views, tree-lined fairways, and the signature Ross greens that require a deft touch. It is a true hidden gem of New England golf.

Nantucket Golf Club is a Rees Jones design that captures the essence of the island's natural beauty. The course features wide fairways, significant bunkering, and a links-like feel. It is known for its exclusive atmosphere and impeccable conditioning.

According to Ted Kennedy Jr., Hyannisport Club’s golf course was what convinced his grandfather, Joseph P. Kennedy, to buy a home there. The course features stunning views of Nantucket Sound and is a classic New England seaside layout with wind-swept fairways and small, challenging greens.

Vineyard Golf Club in Edgarton is notable for being one of the only completely organic golf courses in the United States. Designed by Gil Hanse and Jim Wagner, the course features a natural, rustic look that blends seamlessly with the Martha's Vineyard landscape.

GreatHorse in Hampden is a relatively new addition to the Massachusetts rankings. Formerly Hampden Country Club, it was completely rebuilt by Brian Silva and transformed into a high-end private club with dramatic bunkering, significant elevation changes, and top-tier amenities.

Dedham Country and Polo Club is the only Seth Raynor design in Massachusetts. The course features many of Raynor's signature template holes, such as the Biarritz and the Redan. It is a unique and challenging test of golf with a strong emphasis on strategy and accuracy.

Vesper Country Club in Tyngsboro is a classic Donald Ross design that dates back to the early 20th century. The course is situated on an island in the Merrimack River and features a peaceful, secluded setting with Ross's trademark green complexes and strategic bunkering.

Brae Burn Country Club's Main Course in West Newton is a storied Donald Ross design that has hosted multiple national championships. The course features significant elevation changes, narrow fairways, and challenging greens that require precise approaches.

Longmeadow Country Club is a classic Donald Ross design located in western Massachusetts. The course features rolling fairways, beautiful old-growth trees, and the signature Ross greens that are the hallmark of his architecture. It is a well-regarded test for players of all levels.

Cape Cod National Golf Club in Brewster is a modern Brian Silva design that has quickly become one of the top-ranked courses in the state. The layout features wide fairways, large greens, and a natural feel that takes advantage of the Cape Cod terrain.

Sacconnesset Golf Club: Cape Cod in Falmouth is a Rees Jones design that features a mix of open and wooded holes. The course is known for its challenging layout, beautiful conditioning, and the strong influence of the nearby Atlantic Ocean.

Miacomet Golf Course on Nantucket is a high-end public facility that offers a links-like experience. The course features wide-open fairways, significant bunkering, and wind-swept conditions that reflect the island's natural character. It is consistently ranked among the top public courses in Massachusetts.

Renaissance in Haverhill is a modern Brian Silva design that features a more open, links-inspired look. The course is characterized by its large, undulating greens and creative bunkering. It offers a unique and challenging alternative to the state's traditional parkland layouts.

Belmont Country Club is a classic Donald Ross design that has been carefully restored to its original glory. The course features the signature Ross green complexes and a strategic layout that rewards thoughtful play. It is a long-standing member of the Massachusetts top rankings.

Architect Tripp Davis says this property outside of Boston, with its forested ambiance and rocky, rollercoaster terrain, demanded a more rugged look. That’s what he and shaper Jason Gold gave it, creating expanses of exposed sand and native grasses and bunker edges that look chewed upon. They also added width to the holes, expanded greens and moved tees to make for more meaningful shot options. The Oaks hosted a LIV Golf Invitational Series in 2022.
courses.architect: Tripp Davis (2022 renovation); Thomas Fazio, ASGCA (original)

courses.other_details: Stone wall along holes 1, 4, 8-13, 16 & 18; bunkers up to 6 feet deep; mountainous terrain.
courses.description: Baker Hill sits among the New Hampshire mountains and provides a classic New England golf experience for every golfer. The course offers a great blend of uphill and downhill shots—delivering plenty of variety throughout the course. Golfers must be prepared to work the ball both ways off the tee, as Rees Jones provides an excellent mix of right-to-left and left-to-right holes. The greens feel tucked into the trees, forcing accuracy on the approach. The bunkers are some of the most difficult in the region; some are nearly six feet deep, and all are lined with fescue. One of the most unique quirks is a call back to North Berwick, as a stone wall runs along 1, 4, 8-13, 16 & 18.

courses.other_details: Reclaimed farmland, oak tree-lined fairways, stone walls, signature par-3 17th with Y-shaped green.
courses.description: Designed by the legendary Arnold Palmer in 2002, the Golf Club of New England sits on 450 acres of reclaimed farmland and is said to be one of the staunchest tests of golf in the area. The course is devoid of residential developments and is routed through natural landscape, showcasing stone walls, oak tree-lined fairways and running brooks, creating an aura of tranquility. While the environment provides ample relaxation, the brutish 7,600-yard golf course re-focuses the golfer’s attention with fescue and wetland areas protecting fairways and putting surfaces throughout. The challenge at the Golf Club of New England culminates with the signature par-3 17th, played over a hazard to a Y-shaped green protected by deep bunkers behind it.
courses.course_name: The Golf Club of New England

courses.other_details: Rock outcroppings, 100-foot elevation changes from tee boxes, forested mountains and wetlands environment.
courses.description: Situated in the dense forests near the Maine and New Hampshire border is the Clive Clark-designed Lake Winnipesaukee Golf Club. Set among a unique environment where forested mountains meet dense wetlands, the golf course is home to dramatic rock outcroppings and extensive natural areas that protect fairway landing zones and greens. The club is also renowned for its dramatic elevation changes, with eight tee boxes positioned almost 100 feet above the landing area below, making for numerous picturesque vistas.

Manchester Country Club’s strength comes in its classic, great routing by Donald Ross. Like so many of his designs, Manchester’s green complexes provide much of the character—with many distinct sections and pronounced, bold contours. The back nine is one of the finest in the region with strong variety and dramatic landforms, including the 17th, which has a creek running through the fairway in the run up to the green. The course is tremendously fun and is yet another great showcase of what made Ross so special.
courses.other_details: Bold contours on green complexes, creek on the 17th hole.

courses.description: Just north of Concord, New Hampshire, is the 1920s era gem, Lake Sunapee Country Club. The prestigious course was designed by Donald Ross and is routed through old forests and rolling hills, with open driving areas and undulating green complexes protected by sinister false fronts and challenging greenside bunkering. In recent years, Ron Forse completed an extensive renovation project focused on returning elements from the original design, which came in the form of bunkers being added back into original locations, expanded green complexes and extensive tree removal, bringing back stunning views of the surrounding mountains. Firm-and-fast conditions along with an enjoyable routing for walking make Lake Sunapee a top-class playing experience.
courses.other_details: Sinister false fronts, mountain views, Ron Forse renovation focused on original design restoration.

courses.description: Shadow Creek has the reputation of being one of the most expensive courses built in America, a reputation it embraces. Ranked first in Nevada since its debut in 1991, it recently moved to 24th on America’s 100 Greatest after renovations.

One gets the feeling Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw looked long and hard at this mountain property on the eastern slope of the Carson Range before moving any dirt. It is ranked in the Second 100 Greatest.
Billed as a co-design between Robert Trent Jones Jr. and his famous father, Southern Highlands is a mainstay in the Nevada rankings. It recently fell just shy of the Second 100 Greatest.
Tom Fazio maximized the elevation changes at this Discovery Land Property, providing dramatic views of the Las Vegas Strip. It is right on the heels of the top 200 courses in the country.
Wolf Creek is a fantasy calendar come to life, with holes clinging to stark canyon hillsides and plunging down into green fairways. It is a member of America's 100 Greatest Public Courses.
Debuted in 1998 and was immediately named as the host of the PGA Tour’s Reno-Tahoe Open (now the Barracuda Championship). It is a Jack Nicklaus Signature design.
Nestled in the heart of the Vegas strip, Wynn Golf Club has become a go-to venue for Capital One’s The Match. It is a Tom Fazio design that was rebuilt and reopened in 2019.
One of the great engineering feats in golf, Cascata climbs up and down a steep mountainside with a 418-foot waterfall that runs through the clubhouse. A Rees Jones design.
One of golf’s most televised courses as the annual host of the American Century Championship. The George Fazio design features several holes along the shores of Lake Tahoe.
Opened in 1996 as the first private Jack Nicklaus-designed golf course in Nevada, it features dramatic elevation changes and views of Lake Las Vegas.
About an hour north of Las Vegas, this Jack Nicklaus design was a member of Golf Digest’s 100 Greatest Public Courses. It features wide fairways and challenging green complexes.
Designed by Jay Morrish and David Druzisky in 2001, DragonRidge snakes through the McCullough Mountains with spectacular views of the Las Vegas valley.
Co-hosted the 1996 Shriners Children’s Open where Tiger Woods got his first PGA Tour win. A Bobby Weed and Fuzzy Zoeller design.
A former member of Golf Digest's 100 Greatest Public Courses, this Jack Nicklaus design sits along the shores of Lake Las Vegas.
A Robert Trent Jones Sr. design renovated by Kyle Phillips in the mid-2000s, featuring classic mountain golf with tall pines and views of Lake Tahoe.
A Rees Jones design renovated in 2017, known for its challenging layout through canyons and plateaus overlooking Las Vegas.
A Bobby Weed and Raymond Floyd design that has hosted both PGA Tour and PGA Tour Champions events. Known for its desert-style layout.

courses.course_name: Las Vegas Paiute Golf Resort: Wolf
courses.description: The longest and most difficult of the three Pete Dye courses at the resort, featuring the signature island green 15th hole.

courses.course_name: Las Vegas Paiute Golf Resort: Snow Mountain
courses.description: The first of the three Pete Dye designs at the resort, often considered the most playable with wide fairways and traditional Dye features.
Meandering through canyons and between rocky outcroppings, Conestoga provides a unique experience with dramatic elevation changes. A Gary Panks design.
